figures.caption: Fig. 1: Classification of common defects found in the current dataset. Anomalies are marked in red.
Fig. 2: Architecture of the PaAno+ multivariate time-series anomaly detection system.
Fig. 3: Training workflow of the PaAno+ model.
Table 3 Sensitivity analysis of patch size. The optimal values for each metric in the table are bolded, and the next-best values are underlined. All experimental results are expressed as percentages.
Table 4 Performance of each method on the TSB-AD-U Eval dataset.
Table 5 Performance of each method on the TSB-AD-M Eval dataset.
Table 6 Ablation experiment results. The optimal results for each metric are indicated in bold. All experimental values are presented as percentages (%).
Fig. 4: Parameter sensitivity analysis of the PaAno+ model’s Top-𝑘 values and memory bank size on the TSB-AD-U and TSB-AD-M Eval datasets.
Fig. 5: Sensitivity analysis of the performance of univariate and multivariate time-series anomaly detection with respect to window length 𝑇 . All results are presented as percentages (%).

Table 7 Hyperparameter sensitivity analysis for different combinations of convolutional kernels. The optimal and suboptimal values for each metric are indicated in bold and underlined, respectively. All experimental results are presented as percentages (%).
